# React Image and Background Image Fade

react image and background image fade is a modern React image loading toolkit for images, background images, responsive sources, placeholders (skeleton, blur, color), lazy loading, and fade transitions. Version 2 includes a hook-first API, `<Picture>` for art direction, and helper utilities.

## Installation
`npm install react-image-and-background-image-fade`

## Usage

### 1. Global CSS
Import the styles once in your app entry point (e.g., `layout.tsx` or `App.tsx`):
```tsx
import 'react-image-and-background-image-fade/styles.css'
```

### 2. Standard Responsive Image
Use the `<Image>` component as a drop-in replacement for standard `<img>` tags.
```tsx
import { Image, easings } from 'react-image-and-background-image-fade'

export function MyComponent() {
  return (
    <Image
      src="/photo-1200.jpg"
      alt="Mountain sunrise"
      width={1200}
      height={800}
      srcSet="/photo-640.jpg 640w, /photo-1200.jpg 1200w, /photo-1800.jpg 1800w"
      sizes="(max-width: 768px) 100vw, 50vw"
      placeholder="skeleton"
      fadeType="zoom-blur"
      easing={easings.cinematic}
      duration={1200}
      skeleton={{
        baseColor: '#111318',
        highlightColor: '#242936',
        accentColor: 'rgb(255 61 129 / 38%)'
      }}
      lazy={true}
    />
  )
}
```

### 3. Background Images
Use `<BackgroundImage>` to apply a preloaded, fading background to an element. You can use the `as` prop to render a specific HTML tag, or `asChild` to apply the background behavior to a custom element.
```tsx
import { BackgroundImage } from 'react-image-and-background-image-fade'

export function HeroSection() {
  return (
    <BackgroundImage
      src="/hero.jpg"
      width="100%"
      height="400px"
      fit="cover"
      position="center"
      placeholder="color"
      color="#1a1a1a"
      fadeType="curtain"
      duration={1200}
    >
      <h1>Layered Content Here</h1>
    </BackgroundImage>
  )
}
```

### 4. Picture for Art Direction / Formats
Use `<Picture>` when you need AVIF/WebP fallbacks or multiple crops.
```tsx
import { Picture } from 'react-image-and-background-image-fade'

<Picture
  src="/landscape.jpg"
  alt="Wide landscape"
  width={1200}
  height={800}
  sources={[
    { srcSet: '/landscape.avif 1200w', type: 'image/avif' },
    { srcSet: '/landscape.webp 1200w', type: 'image/webp' }
  ]}
/>
```

### 5. Fade Types and Easings
Eight reveal styles: `fade` (default), `blur-in`, `zoom-blur`, `soft-reveal`, `wipe`, `slide-up`, `scale`, `curtain`.
Curated easing curves: `easings.default`, `material`, `apple`, `emphasized`, `sharp`, `spring`, `cinematic`, `dramatic`.

### 6. Image Hooks and Responsive Helpers
Use `useImage` to build custom loading UIs:
```tsx
import { useImage } from 'react-image-and-background-image-fade'

function CustomLoader() {
  const { status, src, isIdle, isLoading, isLoaded, isError } = useImage({ src: '/heavy.jpg' })
  // status is 'idle' | 'loading' | 'loaded' | 'error'
  
  if (isLoading) return <Spinner />
  if (isError) return <ErrorPlaceholder />
  return <img src={src} />
}
```

Use `createSrcSet` and `createSizes` for cleaner responsive APIs:
```tsx
import { createSizes, createSrcSet } from 'react-image-and-background-image-fade'

const srcSet = createSrcSet({ src: 'https://images.example.com/photo.jpg', widths: [480, 768, 1200, 1600], quality: 80 })
const sizes = createSizes({
  breakpoints: [
    { media: '(max-width: 640px)', size: '100vw' },
    { media: '(max-width: 1200px)', size: '50vw' }
  ],
  defaultSize: '640px'
})
```

Preload helpers are available: `preloadImage({ src, srcSet, sizes })`, `preloadImages(srcArray)`, `preconnectImage(url)`.

### 7. Skeleton Shimmer Customization
Pass a typed `skeleton` object to the component or use CSS variables:
```css
.my-image {
  --ribif-skeleton-bg: #111318;
  --ribif-skeleton-highlight: #242936;
  --ribif-skeleton-accent: rgb(255 61 129 / 38%);
  --ribif-skeleton-sheen: rgb(255 255 255 / 76%);
  --ribif-shimmer-color: rgb(255 255 255 / 46%);
  --ribif-shimmer-speed: 1.1s;
  --ribif-shimmer-angle: 118deg;
  --ribif-shimmer-size: 62%;
  --ribif-skeleton-radius: 18px;
  --ribif-blur-amount: 20px;
}
```

## AI Best Practices
- **Always** include the `styles.css` import in the root.
- Use `width` and `height` props to prevent cumulative layout shift (CLS).
- For placeholders, `blur` can use a `blurDataURL` (passed natively as `src` while loading), but if it is not supplied the package uses the final image as the blurred preview so it still blurs in seamlessly.
- Use `placeholder="blur"` with `fadeType="blur-in"` or `fadeType="zoom-blur"` with `easing={easings.cinematic}` for the most cinematic loading experience.
- Use `<Picture>` for AVIF/WebP image fallbacks.
- Use `ImageConfigProvider` to set consistent defaults across your app.
